2603.15057.title: Analyzing Error Sources in Global Feature Effect Estimation
2603.15057.paper_summary: Global feature effects such as partial dependence (PD) and accumulated local effects (ALE) plots are widely used to interpret black-box models. However, they are only estimates of true underlying effects, and their reliability depends on multiple sources of error. Despite the popularity of global feature effects, these error sources are largely unexplored. In particular, the practically relevant question of whether to use training or holdout data to estimate feature effects remains unanswered. We address this gap by providing a systematic, estimator-level analysis that disentangles sources of bias and variance for PD and ALE. To this end, we derive a mean-squared-error decomposition that separates model bias, estimation bias, model variance, and estimation variance, and analyze their dependence on model characteristics, data selection, and sample size. We validate our theoretical findings through an extensive simulation study across multiple data-generating processes, learners, estimation strategies (training data, validation data, and cross-validation), and sample sizes. Our results reveal that, while using holdout data is theoretically the cleanest, potential biases arising from the training data are empirically negligible and dominated by the impact of the usually higher sample size. The estimation variance depends on both the presence of interactions and the sample size, with ALE being particularly sensitive to the latter. Cross-validation-based estimation is a promising approach that reduces the model variance component, particularly for overfitting models. Our analysis provides a principled explanation of the sources of error in feature effect estimates and offers concrete guidance on choosing estimation strategies when interpreting machine learning models.
2603.15057.paper_summary_zh: 全球特徵效應，如部分依賴（PD）和累積局部效應（ALE）圖，廣泛用於解釋黑箱模型。<lb/>然而，它們僅是對真實潛在效應的估計，其可靠性取決於多個錯誤來源。<lb/>儘管全球特徵效應受到廣泛關注，但這些錯誤來源在很大程度上尚未被探討。<lb/>特別是，是否使用訓練數據或保留數據來估計特徵效應的實際相關問題仍然沒有答案。<lb/>我們通過提供一個系統的估計器級別分析來解決這一空白，該分析解開了PD和ALE的偏差和方差來源。<lb/>為此，我們推導出一個均方誤差分解，將模型偏差、估計偏差、模型方差和估計方差分開，並分析它們對模型特徵、數據選擇和樣本大小的依賴性。<lb/>我們通過在多個數據生成過程、學習者、估計策略（訓練數據、驗證數據和交叉驗證）和樣本大小下進行的廣泛模擬研究來驗證我們的理論發現。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，雖然使用保留數據在理論上是最乾淨的，但由訓練數據引起的潛在偏差在實證上是微不足道的，並且被通常較高的樣本大小的影響所主導。<lb/>估計方差取決於交互作用的存在和樣本大小，其中ALE對後者特別敏感。<lb/>基於交叉驗證的估計是一種有前景的方法，可以減少模型方差成分，特別是對於過擬合模型。<lb/>我們的分析提供了特徵效應估計中錯誤來源的原則性解釋，並在解釋機器學習模型時提供了選擇估計策略的具體指導。
2603.15057.author: Timo Heiß et.al.
2603.15057.authors: Timo Heiß, Coco Bögel, Bernd Bischl, Giuseppe Casalicchio
